text: This paper introduces a novel framework for human pose estimation leveraging Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). By formulating pose estimation as a direct regression problem targeting body joint coordinates, we develop a cascade of DNN regressors that achieves high-precision localization. Our approach offers the distinct advantage of holistic pose reasoning, utilizing a streamlined yet robust architecture that captures complex spatial dependencies by capitalizing on recent breakthroughs in deep learning. We provide a comprehensive empirical evaluation demonstrating that our method achieves state-of-the-art results across four diverse, real-world academic benchmarks.
